 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 6	import org.springframework.http.HttpMethod;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 8	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity;
 9	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.configuration.EnableWebSecurity;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.configurers.AbstractHttpConfigurer;
 11	import org.springframework.security.config.http.SessionCreationPolicy;
 12	import org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain;
 13	
 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 43	
 44	 @PostMapping("/introspect")
 45	 public ResponseEntity<?> introspect(@RequestHeader(value = "Authorization", required = false) String authorizationHeader) {
 46	 String accessToken = null;
 47	 if (authorizationHeader != null && authorizationHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
 48	 accessToken = authorizationHeader.substring(7);
 49	 }
 50	
 51	 boolean isValid = jwtService.isTokenValid(accessToken);
 52	 return ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of("valid", isValid));
 53	 }

 21	@REM ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 22	@REM Apache Maven Wrapper startup batch script, version 3.3.2
 23	@REM
 24	@REM Optional ENV vars
 25	@REM MVNW_REPOURL - repo url base for downloading maven distribution
 26	@REM MVNW_USERNAME/MVNW_PASSWORD - user and password for downloading maven
 27	@REM MVNW_VERBOSE - true: enable verbose log; others: silence the output
 28	@REM ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 29	
 30	@IF "%__MVNW_ARG0_NAME__%"=="" (SET __MVNW_ARG0_NAME__=%~nx0)
 31	@SET __MVNW_CMD__=
 32	@SET __MVNW_ERROR__=
 33	@SET __MVNW_PSMODULEP_SAVE=%PSModulePath%
 34	@SET PSModulePath=
 35	@FOR /F "usebackq tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (`powershell -noprofile "& {$scriptDir='%~dp0'; $script='%__MVNW_ARG0_NAME__%'; icm -ScriptBlock ([Scriptblock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw '%~f0'))) -NoNewScope}"`) DO @(
 36	 IF "%%A"=="MVN_CMD" (set __MVNW_CMD__=%%B) ELSE IF "%%B"=="" (echo %%A) ELSE (echo %%A=%%B)
 37	)

 58	
 59	switch -wildcard -casesensitive ( $($distributionUrl -replace '^.*/','') ) {
 60	 "maven-mvnd-*" {
 61	 $USE_MVND = $true
 62	 $distributionUrl = $distributionUrl -replace '-bin\.[^.]*$',"-windows-amd64.zip"
 63	 $MVN_CMD = "mvnd.cmd"
 64	 break
 65	 }
 66	 default {
 67	 $USE_MVND = $false
 68	 $MVN_CMD = $script -replace '^mvnw','mvn'
 69	 break
 70	 }
 71	}
 72	
 73	# apply MVNW_REPOURL and calculate MAVEN_HOME
 74	# maven home pattern: ~/.m2/wrapper/dists/{apache-maven-<version>,maven-mvnd-<version>-<platform>}/<hash>
 75	if ($env:MVNW_REPOURL) {
 76	 $MVNW_REPO_PATTERN = if ($USE_MVND) { "/org/apache/maven/" } else { "/maven/mvnd/" }
 77	 $distributionUrl = "$env:MVNW_REPOURL$MVNW_REPO_PATTERN$($distributionUrl -replace '^.*'+$MVNW_REPO_PATTERN,'')"
 78	}
 79	$distributionUrlName = $distributionUrl -replace '^.*/',''
 80	$distributionUrlNameMain = $distributionUrlName -replace '\.[^.]*$','' -replace '-bin$',''
 81	$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$HOME/.m2/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 82	if ($env:MAVEN_USER_HOME) {
 83	 $MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$env:MAVEN_USER_HOME/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 84	}
 85	$MAVEN_HOME_NAME = ([System.Security.Cryptography.MD5]::Create().ComputeHash([byte[]][char[]]$distributionUrl) | ForEach-Object {$_.ToString("x2")}) -join ''
 86	$MAVEN_HOME = "$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT/$MAVEN_HOME_NAME"
 87	
 88	if (Test-Path -Path "$MAVEN_HOME" -PathType Container) {
 89	 Write-Verbose "found existing MAVEN_HOME at $MAVEN_HOME"
 90	 Write-Output "MVN_CMD=$MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD"
 91	 exit $?
 92	}

 20	
 21	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 22	# Apache Maven Wrapper startup batch script, version 3.3.2
 23	#
 24	# Optional ENV vars
 25	# -----------------
 26	# JAVA_HOME - location of a JDK home dir, required when download maven via java source
 27	# MVNW_REPOURL - repo url base for downloading maven distribution
 28	# MVNW_USERNAME/MVNW_PASSWORD - user and password for downloading maven
 29	# MVNW_VERBOSE - true: enable verbose log; debug: trace the mvnw script; others: silence the output
 30	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 31	
 32	set -euf
 33	[ "${MVNW_VERBOSE-}" != debug ] || set -x
...
 107	
 108	# parse distributionUrl and optional distributionSha256Sum, requires .mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties
 109	while IFS="=" read -r key value; do
 110	 case "${key-}" in
 111	 distributionUrl) distributionUrl=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 112	 distributionSha256Sum) distributionSha256Sum=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 113	 esac
 114	done <"${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"
 115	[ -n "${distributionUrl-}" ] || die "cannot read distributionUrl property in ${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"
 116	
 117	case "${distributionUrl##*/}" in
 118	maven-mvnd-*bin.*)
 119	 MVN_CMD=mvnd.sh _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/maven/mvnd/
 120	 case "${PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE-}${PROCESSOR_ARCHITEW6432-}:$(uname -a)" in
 121	 *AMD64:CYGWIN* | *AMD64:MINGW*) distributionPlatform=windows-amd64 ;;
 122	 :Darwin*x86_64) distributionPlatform=darwin-amd64 ;;
 123	 :Darwin*arm64) distributionPlatform=darwin-aarch64 ;;
 124	 :Linux*x86_64*) distributionPlatform=linux-amd64 ;;
 125	 *)
 126	 echo "Cannot detect native platform for mvnd on $(uname)-$(uname -m), use pure java version" >&2
 127	 distributionPlatform=linux-amd64
 128	 ;;
 129	 esac
 130	 distributionUrl="${distributionUrl%-bin.*}-$distributionPlatform.zip"
 131	 ;;
 132	maven-mvnd-*) MVN_CMD=mvnd.sh _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/maven/mvnd/ ;;
 133	*) MVN_CMD="mvn${0##*/mvnw}" _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/org/apache/maven/ ;;
 134	esac
 135	
 136	# apply MVNW_REPOURL and calculate MAVEN_HOME
 137	# maven home pattern: ~/.m2/wrapper/dists/{apache-maven-<version>,maven-mvnd-<version>-<platform>}/<hash>
 138	[ -z "${MVNW_REPOURL-}" ] || distributionUrl="$MVNW_REPOURL$_MVNW_REPO_PATTERN${distributionUrl#*"$_MVNW_REPO_PATTERN"}"
 139	distributionUrlName="${distributionUrl##*/}"
 140	distributionUrlNameMain="${distributionUrlName%.*}"
 141	distributionUrlNameMain="${distributionUrlNameMain%-bin}"
 142	MAVEN_USER_HOME="${MAVEN_USER_HOME:-${HOME}/.m2}"
 143	MAVEN_HOME="${MAVEN_USER_HOME}/wrapper/dists/${distributionUrlNameMain-}/$(hash_string "$distributionUrl")"
 144	
 145	exec_maven() {
 146	 unset MVNW_VERBOSE MVNW_USERNAME MVNW_PASSWORD MVNW_REPOURL || :
 147	 exec "$MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD" "$@" || die "cannot exec $MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD"
 148	}

 1	package org.endipi.auth.resource;
 2	
 3	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;
 4	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 5	import org.endipi.auth.dto.request.AuthRequest;
 6	import org.endipi.auth.dto.request.ChangePasswordRequest;
 7	import org.endipi.auth.dto.response.ApiResponse;
 8	import org.endipi.auth.dto.response.AuthResponse;
 9	import org.endipi.auth.service.AuthService;
 10	import org.endipi.auth.util.JwtUtil;
 11	import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
 12	import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
 13	import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
 14	
 15	import java.util.Map;
 16	
 17	@RestController
 18	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 19	@RequestMapping("/api/v1/auth")
 20	public class AuthResource {
 21	 private final AuthService authenticationService;
 22	 private final JwtUtil jwtService;
 23	
 24	 @PostMapping("/login")
 25	 public AuthResponse login(@RequestBody(required = false) AuthRequest authRequest, HttpServletResponse response) {
 26	 return authenticationService.login(authRequest, response);
 27	 }
 28	
 29	 @PostMapping("/logout")
 30	 public ResponseEntity<?> logout(
 31	 @RequestHeader(name = "Authorization", required = false) String authorizationHeader,
 32	 @CookieValue(name = "refreshToken", required = false) String refreshToken,
 33	 HttpServletResponse response) {
 34	
 35	 String accessToken = null;
 36	 if (authorizationHeader != null && authorizationHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
 37	 accessToken = authorizationHeader.substring(7);
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 authenticationService.logout(accessToken, refreshToken, response);
 41	 return ResponseEntity.ok().body(Map.of("message", "Logged out successfully"));
 42	 }
 43	
 44	 @PostMapping("/introspect")
 45	 public ResponseEntity<?> introspect(@RequestHeader(value = "Authorization", required = false) String authorizationHeader) {
 46	 String accessToken = null;
 47	 if (authorizationHeader != null && authorizationHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
 48	 accessToken = authorizationHeader.substring(7);
 49	 }
 50	
 51	 boolean isValid = jwtService.isTokenValid(accessToken);
 52	 return ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of("valid", isValid));
 53	 }
 54	
 55	 @PostMapping("/refresh")
 56	 public ResponseEntity<?> refresh(@CookieValue(name = "refreshToken", required = false) String refreshToken,
 57	 HttpServletResponse response) {
 58	 if (refreshToken == null) {
 59	 return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED).body(Map.of("message", "Refresh token missing"));
 60	 }
 61	
 62	 return ResponseEntity.ok(authenticationService.refresh(refreshToken, response));
 63	 }
 64	
 65	 @PutMapping("/change-password")
 66	 public ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<String, String>> changePassword(
 67	 @RequestBody ChangePasswordRequest request,
 68	 @RequestHeader("Authorization") String token
 69	 ) {
 70	 token = token.substring(7);
 71	 String email = jwtService.extractUsername(token);
 72	
 73	 authenticationService.changePassword(email, request);
 74	 ApiResponse<String, String> response = ApiResponse.<String, String>builder()
 75	 .timestamp(System.currentTimeMillis())
 76	 .statusCode(HttpStatus.OK.value())
 77	 .message("Đổi mật khẩu thành công!")
 78	 .build();
 79	 return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.OK).body(response);
 80	 }
 81	
 82	}...

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.endipi.auth.repository.UserRepository;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 6	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
 8	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 9	import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
 11	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
 12	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException;
 13	import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
 14	import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
 15	
 16	@Configuration
 17	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 18	public class AppConfig {
 19	 private final UserRepository userRepository;
 20	
 21	 @Bean
 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {
 23	 return username -> userRepository.findByEmail(username)
 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}

 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.endipi.auth.repository.UserRepository;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 6	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
 8	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 9	import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
 11	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
 12	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException;
 13	import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
 14	import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
 15	
 16	@Configuration
 17	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 18	public class AppConfig {
 19	 private final UserRepository userRepository;
 20	
 21	 @Bean
 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {
 23	 return username -> userRepository.findByEmail(username)
 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"c7d85942-2973-4172-a19d-12ab0e9a67c4","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"Based on my analysis of your microservices setup, I've identified several potential reasons why accessing `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login` through the API Gateway is returning a 404 error. Let me provide you with a comprehensive assessment:

## **Analysis Summary**

Your microservices architecture consists of:
- **Config Server** (port 8888)
- **Discovery Service** (Eureka Server, port 8761) 
- **Gateway Service** (port 8222)
- **Auth Service** (port 8000)

## **Identified Issues**

### **1. Missing Eureka Client Configuration**

The most critical issue is that **both the Gateway and Auth services are missing Eureka client configuration**. While they have the Eureka client dependencies in their `pom.xml` files, they lack the necessary Eureka client settings.

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes:
 - id: auth-service
 uri: lb://AUTH-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/auth/**
```
</augment_code_snippet>

**Missing Eureka client configuration for Gateway:**
```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
spring:
 autoconfigure:
 exclude: org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.security.servlet.UserDetailsServiceAutoConfiguration
 datasource:
 url: jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/auth_service?createDatabaseIfNotExist=true
 username: root
 password: root
```
</augment_code_snippet>

**Missing Eureka client configuration for Auth Service:**
```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

### **2. Missing @EnableEurekaClient Annotations**

The Gateway and Auth service main application classes are missing the `@EnableEurekaClient` annotation:

<augment_code_snippet path="services/gateway/src/main/java/org/endipi/gateway/GatewayApplication.java" mode="EXCERPT">
```java
@SpringBootApplication
public class GatewayApplication {
 public static void main(String[] args) {
 SpringApplication.run(GatewayApplication.class, args);
 }
}
```
</augment_code_snippet>

<augment_code_snippet path="services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/AuthApplication.java" mode="EXCERPT">
```java
@SpringBootApplication
public class AuthApplication {
 public static void main(String[] args) {
 SpringApplication.run(AuthApplication.class, args);
 }
}
```
</augment_code_snippet>

### **3. Gateway Configuration Issue**

The gateway configuration has a structural issue. The `routes` should be directly under `gateway`, not under `server.webflux`:

**Current (incorrect) structure:**
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This is incorrectly nested
```

**Should be:**
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This should be directly under gateway
```

## **Root Cause Analysis**

The 404 error occurs because:

1. **Service Discovery Failure**: The Auth service is not registering with Eureka due to missing client configuration
2. **Gateway Cannot Find Service**: The Gateway cannot locate the `AUTH-SERVICE` instance via load balancer (`lb://AUTH-SERVICE`) because it's not registered in Eureka
3. **Route Resolution Failure**: When you hit `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login`, the Gateway tries to route to `lb://AUTH-SERVICE` but fails to resolve it

## **Verification Steps**

To confirm these issues, you should check:

1. **Eureka Dashboard**: Visit `http://localhost:8761` to see if the AUTH-SERVICE and GATEWAY-SERVICE are registered
2. **Service Logs**: Check the startup logs of both Gateway and Auth services for Eureka registration messages
3. **Direct Auth Service Access**: Try accessing `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login` directly to confirm the auth service is running

## **Expected Behavior After Fixes**

Once the Eureka client configurations are added:
1. Auth service will register itself as `AUTH-SERVICE` in Eureka
2. Gateway service will register itself and be able to discover other services
3. Gateway will successfully route `/api/v1/auth/**` requests to the Auth service
4. Your `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login` request should work

The auth endpoint itself looks correctly configured with the `@PostMapping("/login")` annotation and proper request mapping.

Would you like me to help you verify these issues by checking the Eureka dashboard or service logs?"},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":"Based on my analysis of your microservices setup, I've identified several potential reasons why accessing `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login` through the API Gateway is returning a 404 error. Let me provide you with a comprehensive assessment:

## **Analysis Summary**

Your microservices architecture consists of:
- **Config Server** (port 8888)
- **Discovery Service** (Eureka Server, port 8761) 
- **Gateway Service** (port 8222)
- **Auth Service** (port 8000)

## **Identified Issues**

### **1. Missing Eureka Client Configuration**

The most critical issue is that **both the Gateway and Auth services are missing Eureka client configuration**. While they have the Eureka client dependencies in their `pom.xml` files, they lack the necessary Eureka client settings.

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes:
 - id: auth-service
 uri: lb://AUTH-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/auth/**
````

**Missing Eureka client configuration for Gateway:**
```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
spring:
 autoconfigure:
 exclude: org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.security.servlet.UserDetailsServiceAutoConfiguration
 datasource:
 url: jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/auth_service?createDatabaseIfNotExist=true
 username: root
 password: root
````

**Missing Eureka client configuration for Auth Service:**
```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

### **2. Missing @EnableEurekaClient Annotations**

The Gateway and Auth service main application classes are missing the `@EnableEurekaClient` annotation:

````java path=services/gateway/src/main/java/org/endipi/gateway/GatewayApplication.java mode=EXCERPT
@SpringBootApplication
public class GatewayApplication {
 public static void main(String[] args) {
 SpringApplication.run(GatewayApplication.class, args);
 }
}
````

````java path=services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/AuthApplication.java mode=EXCERPT
@SpringBootApplication
public class AuthApplication {
 public static void main(String[] args) {
 SpringApplication.run(AuthApplication.class, args);
 }
}
````

### **3. Gateway Configuration Issue**

The gateway configuration has a structural issue. The `routes` should be directly under `gateway`, not under `server.webflux`:

**Current (incorrect) structure:**
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This is incorrectly nested
```

**Should be:**
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This should be directly under gateway
```

## **Root Cause Analysis**

The 404 error occurs because:

1. **Service Discovery Failure**: The Auth service is not registering with Eureka due to missing client configuration
2. **Gateway Cannot Find Service**: The Gateway cannot locate the `AUTH-SERVICE` instance via load balancer (`lb://AUTH-SERVICE`) because it's not registered in Eureka
3. **Route Resolution Failure**: When you hit `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login`, the Gateway tries to route to `lb://AUTH-SERVICE` but fails to resolve it

## **Verification Steps**

To confirm these issues, you should check:

1. **Eureka Dashboard**: Visit `http://localhost:8761` to see if the AUTH-SERVICE and GATEWAY-SERVICE are registered
2. **Service Logs**: Check the startup logs of both Gateway and Auth services for Eureka registration messages
3. **Direct Auth Service Access**: Try accessing `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login` directly to confirm the auth service is running

## **Expected Behavior After Fixes**

Once the Eureka client configurations are added:
1. Auth service will register itself as `AUTH-SERVICE` in Eureka
2. Gateway service will register itself and be able to discover other services
3. Gateway will successfully route `/api/v1/auth/**` requests to the Auth service
4. Your `localhost:8222/api/v1/auth/login` request should work

The auth endpoint itself looks correctly configured with the `@PostMapping("/login")` annotation and proper request mapping.

Would you like me to help you verify these issues by checking the Eureka dashboard or service logs?

 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {

 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}

 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 10	@Component
 11	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 12	public class UserEventProducer {
 13	 private final KafkaTemplate<String, Object> kafkaTemplate;
 14	
 15	 @Value("${kafka.topic.user-events}")
 16	 private String userEventsTopic;
 17	
 18	 public void publishUserCreated(User user) {
 19	 UserEvent event = UserEvent.builder()
 20	 .eventType("CREATE")
 21	 .userId(user.getId())
 22	 .email(user.getEmail())
 23	 .password(user.getPassword())
 24	 .role(user.getRole().getRoleTitle().name())
 25	 .timestamp(System.currentTimeMillis())
 26	 .build();
 27	
 28	 kafkaTemplate.send(userEventsTopic, user.getId().toString(), event);
 29	 }

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"f7c17988-6faf-4bbc-817c-5a9824c81ff5","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"## **Updated Codebase Review**

After reviewing your updated codebase, I can see several improvements you've made, but the **core issue causing the 404 error remains unchanged**. Here's what I found:

### **What You've Added/Updated:**

1. **New User Service**: You've added a complete User service with:
 - User, Student, Teacher, Role entities
 - Kafka integration for user events
 - Email functionality
 - Port 8001 configuration

2. **Kafka Integration**: Both Auth and User services now have Kafka configuration for event-driven communication

3. **Enhanced Auth Service**: 
 - Changed from `UserRepository` to `AuthUserRepository`
 - Added Kafka consumer for user events
 - Updated JWT secret key

4. **Gateway Routing**: Added routing for the new User service:

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
routes:
 - id: auth-service
 uri: lb://AUTH-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/auth/**
 - id: user-service
 uri: lb://USER-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/user/**
```
</augment_code_snippet>

5. **Docker Compose**: Added Kafka and Zookeeper services

### **Critical Issues Still Present:**

Unfortunately, **the same fundamental issues that cause the 404 error are still present**:

#### **1. Missing Eureka Client Configuration**

All your services (Gateway, Auth, User) still lack Eureka client configuration. The configuration files are missing:

```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

#### **2. Missing @EnableEurekaClient Annotations**

Your main application classes still don't have the required annotations:

<augment_code_snippet path="services/gateway/src/main/java/org/endipi/gateway/GatewayApplication.java" mode="EXCERPT">
```java
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class GatewayApplication {
```
</augment_code_snippet>

<augment_code_snippet path="services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/AuthApplication.java" mode="EXCERPT">
```java
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class AuthApplication {
```
</augment_code_snippet>

<augment_code_snippet path="services/user/src/main/java/org/endipi/user/UserApplication.java" mode="EXCERPT">
```java
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class UserApplication {
```
</augment_code_snippet>

#### **3. Gateway Configuration Structure Issue**

The gateway routing configuration still has the incorrect structure:

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This should be directly under 'gateway'
```
</augment_code_snippet>

### **Root Cause Analysis - Still the Same:**

The 404 error occurs because:

1. **Services Not Registering**: Auth and User services aren't registering with Eureka
2. **Gateway Can't Discover Services**: Gateway can't find `AUTH-SERVICE` or `USER-SERVICE` instances
3. **Load Balancer Fails**: `lb://AUTH-SERVICE` resolution fails

### **What You Need to Fix:**

1. **Add Eureka client configuration** to `gateway-service.yml`, `auth-service.yml`, and `user-service.yml`
2. **Add @EnableEurekaClient** annotations to Gateway, Auth, and User application classes
3. **Fix gateway routing structure** in the configuration
4. **Verify service registration** by checking the Eureka dashboard at `http://localhost:8761`

### **Positive Observations:**

- Your microservices architecture is well-designed with proper separation of concerns
- Kafka integration shows good understanding of event-driven architecture
- Configuration management through Config Server is properly implemented
- Dependencies are correctly configured in all `pom.xml` files

The core infrastructure is solid, but the service discovery configuration is the missing piece preventing the gateway from routing requests properly.

After reviewing your updated codebase, I can see several improvements you've made, but the **core issue causing the 404 error remains unchanged**. Here's what I found:

### **What You've Added/Updated:**

1. **New User Service**: You've added a complete User service with:
 - User, Student, Teacher, Role entities
 - Kafka integration for user events
 - Email functionality
 - Port 8001 configuration

2. **Kafka Integration**: Both Auth and User services now have Kafka configuration for event-driven communication

3. **Enhanced Auth Service**: 
 - Changed from `UserRepository` to `AuthUserRepository`
 - Added Kafka consumer for user events
 - Updated JWT secret key

4. **Gateway Routing**: Added routing for the new User service:

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
routes:
 - id: auth-service
 uri: lb://AUTH-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/auth/**
 - id: user-service
 uri: lb://USER-SERVICE
 predicates:
 - Path=/api/v1/user/**
````

5. **Docker Compose**: Added Kafka and Zookeeper services

### **Critical Issues Still Present:**

Unfortunately, **the same fundamental issues that cause the 404 error are still present**:

#### **1. Missing Eureka Client Configuration**

All your services (Gateway, Auth, User) still lack Eureka client configuration. The configuration files are missing:

```yaml
eureka:
 client:
 service-url:
 defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
 fetch-registry: true
 register-with-eureka: true
```

#### **2. Missing @EnableEurekaClient Annotations**

Your main application classes still don't have the required annotations:

````java path=services/gateway/src/main/java/org/endipi/gateway/GatewayApplication.java mode=EXCERPT
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class GatewayApplication {
````

````java path=services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/AuthApplication.java mode=EXCERPT
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class AuthApplication {
````

````java path=services/user/src/main/java/org/endipi/user/UserApplication.java mode=EXCERPT
@SpringBootApplication // Missing @EnableEurekaClient
public class UserApplication {
````

#### **3. Gateway Configuration Structure Issue**

The gateway routing configuration still has the incorrect structure:

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/gateway-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
spring:
 cloud:
 gateway:
 server:
 webflux:
 discovery:
 locator:
 enabled: true
 routes: # This should be directly under 'gateway'
````

### **Root Cause Analysis - Still the Same:**

The 404 error occurs because:

1. **Services Not Registering**: Auth and User services aren't registering with Eureka
2. **Gateway Can't Discover Services**: Gateway can't find `AUTH-SERVICE` or `USER-SERVICE` instances
3. **Load Balancer Fails**: `lb://AUTH-SERVICE` resolution fails

### **What You Need to Fix:**

1. **Add Eureka client configuration** to `gateway-service.yml`, `auth-service.yml`, and `user-service.yml`
2. **Add @EnableEurekaClient** annotations to Gateway, Auth, and User application classes
3. **Fix gateway routing structure** in the configuration
4. **Verify service registration** by checking the Eureka dashboard at `http://localhost:8761`

### **Positive Observations:**

- Your microservices architecture is well-designed with proper separation of concerns
- Kafka integration shows good understanding of event-driven architecture
- Configuration management through Config Server is properly implemented
- Dependencies are correctly configured in all `pom.xml` files

The core infrastructure is solid, but the service discovery configuration is the missing piece preventing the gateway from routing requests properly.

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"82ce0679-47a4-4f89-87e8-62139ae541dd","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"Perfect! I found the exact issue. You have a **configuration mismatch** between your producer and consumer configurations. Let me break down what's happening:

## **Root Cause Analysis**

### **The Problem:**

1. **Producer Configuration (User Service)** - You're setting:
 ```yaml
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
 ```

2. **Consumer Configuration (Auth Service)** - You're expecting:
 ```yaml
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
 ```

### **What's Happening:**

When you send a message from User Service, the `JsonSerializer` is **NOT adding type headers** because you set `spring.json.add.type.headers: false`. However, your Auth Service consumer is trying to deserialize the message and **expects to find class type information** to map `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` to `org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent`.

Since there are no type headers in the message, the `JsonDeserializer` tries to find the class `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` in the Auth Service classpath, but **it doesn't exist there** - it only exists in the User Service!

## **The Configuration Conflict:**

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/user-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false # Producer NOT sending type info
```
</augment_code_snippet>

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false # Consumer NOT using type headers
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```
</augment_code_snippet>

## **Two Solutions:**

### **Solution 1: Use Type Headers (Recommended)**

**Change User Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: true # Enable type headers
```

**Change Auth Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: true # Use type headers
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

### **Solution 2: Use Default Type (Alternative)**

**Keep User Service as is:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

**Change Auth Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 # Remove the type.mapping line
```

## **Why This Happens:**

1. **Producer sends JSON** without class type information
2. **Consumer receives JSON** but doesn't know what class to deserialize it to
3. **JsonDeserializer tries to find** the original class (`org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent`) 
4. **Class not found** in Auth Service classpath → **SerializationException**
5. **ErrorHandlingDeserializer** can't handle this type of exception → **IllegalStateException**

## **Recommended Fix:**

I recommend **Solution 1** because it's more explicit and maintainable. The type headers ensure that even if you change the class structure later, the mapping will still work correctly.

Would you like me to help you implement one of these solutions?

 21	 .userId(user.getId())
 22	 .email(user.getEmail())
 23	 .password(user.getPassword())
 24	 .role(user.getRole().getRoleTitle().name())
 25	 .timestamp(System.currentTimeMillis())
 26	 .build();
 27	
 28	 kafkaTemplate.send(userEventsTopic, user.getId().toString(), event);
 29	 }

Path: services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/consumer/UserEventConsumer.java
 1	package org.endipi.auth.consumer;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
 5	import org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent;
 6	import org.endipi.auth.entity.AuthUser;
 7	import org.endipi.auth.repository.AuthUserRepository;
 8	import org.springframework.kafka.annotation.KafkaListener;
 9	import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
 10	
 11	@Slf4j
 12	@Service
 13	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 14	public class UserEventConsumer {
 15	 private final AuthUserRepository authUserRepository;
 16	
 17	 @KafkaListener(topics = "${kafka.topic.user-events}")
 18	 public void handleUserEvent(UserEvent event) {
 19	 log.info("Received user event: {}", event);
 20	
 21	 try {
 22	 switch (event.getEventType()) {
 23	 case "CREATE":
 24	 handleUserCreated(event);
 25	 break;
 26	 case "UPDATE":
 27	 handleUserUpdated(event);
 28	 break;
 29	 case "DELETE":
 30	 handleUserDeleted(event);
 31	 break;
 32	 default:
 33	 log.warn("Unknown event type: {}", event.getEventType());
 34	 }
 35	 } catch (Exception e) {
 36	 log.error("Error processing user event: {}", event, e);
 37	 // Implement error handling strategy (retry, DLQ, etc.)
 38	 }
 39	 }
 40	
 41	 private void handleUserCreated(UserEvent event) {
 42	 AuthUser authUser = AuthUser.builder()
 43	 .email(event.getEmail())
 44	 .password(event.getPassword())
 45	 .role(event.getRole())
 46	 .build();
 47	 authUserRepository.save(authUser);
 48	 }
 49	
 50	 private void handleUserUpdated(UserEvent event) {
 51	 authUserRepository.findByEmail(event.getEmail())
 52	 .ifPresent(authUser -> {
 53	 authUser.setPassword(event.getPassword());
 54	 authUser.setRole(event.getRole());
 55	 authUserRepository.save(authUser);
 56	 });
 57	 }
 58	
 59	 private void handleUserDeleted(UserEvent event) {
 60	 authUserRepository.findByEmail(event.getEmail())
 61	 .ifPresent(authUserRepository::delete);
 62	 }
 63	}
...

I found the exact issue. You have a **configuration mismatch** between your producer and consumer configurations. Let me break down what's happening:

## **Root Cause Analysis**

### **The Problem:**

1. **Producer Configuration (User Service)** - You're setting:
 ```yaml
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
 ```

2. **Consumer Configuration (Auth Service)** - You're expecting:
 ```yaml
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
 ```

### **What's Happening:**

When you send a message from User Service, the `JsonSerializer` is **NOT adding type headers** because you set `spring.json.add.type.headers: false`. However, your Auth Service consumer is trying to deserialize the message and **expects to find class type information** to map `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` to `org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent`.

Since there are no type headers in the message, the `JsonDeserializer` tries to find the class `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` in the Auth Service classpath, but **it doesn't exist there** - it only exists in the User Service!

## **The Configuration Conflict:**

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/user-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false # Producer NOT sending type info
````

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false # Consumer NOT using type headers
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
````

## **Two Solutions:**

### **Solution 1: Use Type Headers (Recommended)**

**Change User Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: true # Enable type headers
```

**Change Auth Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: true # Use type headers
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

### **Solution 2: Use Default Type (Alternative)**

**Keep User Service as is:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

**Change Auth Service configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 # Remove the type.mapping line
```

## **Why This Happens:**

1. **Producer sends JSON** without class type information
2. **Consumer receives JSON** but doesn't know what class to deserialize it to
3. **JsonDeserializer tries to find** the original class (`org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent`) 
4. **Class not found** in Auth Service classpath → **SerializationException**
5. **ErrorHandlingDeserializer** can't handle this type of exception → **IllegalStateException**

## **Recommended Fix:**

I recommend **Solution 1** because it's more explicit and maintainable. The type headers ensure that even if you change the class structure later, the mapping will still work correctly.

Would you like me to help you implement one of these solutions?

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"9293a7cb-f13c-41a6-8539-af965e6f093a","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"Exactly! You've identified the core issue perfectly. The problem is in line 26 of your auth-service.yml:

<augment_code_snippet path="services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml" mode="EXCERPT">
```yaml
spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```
</augment_code_snippet>

This configuration is telling the deserializer: "When you see a message with type `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent`, map it to `org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent`". But the auth-service has **no knowledge** of `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` class!

## **The Correct Solutions:**

### **Solution 1: Remove Type Mapping Completely (Simplest)**

Since both your `UserEvent` classes have identical structure, just remove the type mapping and use the default type:

**Auth Service Configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 # Remove this line completely:
 # spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

**User Service Configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

### **Solution 2: Use Custom Type Mapping with Alias (More Advanced)**

**User Service - Send with custom type alias:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: true
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:userEvent"
```

**Auth Service - Map alias to local class:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: true
 spring.json.type.mapping: "userEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

### **Solution 3: Shared Event Library (Best Practice)**

Create a separate `events-common` module that both services depend on:

```
events-common/
 └── src/main/java/org/endipi/events/UserEvent.java
```

Both services would then use the same `UserEvent` class.

## **Recommended Fix (Solution 1):**

Update your `auth-service.yml`:

```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 bootstrap-servers: localhost:9092
 group-id: auth-service-group
 key-deserializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.ErrorHandlingDeserializer
 value-deserializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.ErrorHandlingDeserializer
 properties:
 spring.json.trusted.packages: "*"
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 spring.deserializer.key.delegate.class: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
 spring.deserializer.value.delegate.class: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonDeserializer
```

And update your `user-service.yml`:

```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 bootstrap-servers: localhost:9092
 key-serializer: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
 value-serializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonSerializer
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

This way, the producer sends pure JSON without type information, and the consumer always deserializes it to its local `UserEvent` class.

Would you like to try Solution 1 first since it's the simplest?

 3	import org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonDeserializer;
 4	import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer;
 5	import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerConfig;
 6	import org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent;
 7	import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;
 8	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 9	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 10	import org.springframework.kafka.annotation.EnableKafka;
 11	import org.springframework.kafka.config.ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory;
 12	import org.springframework.kafka.core.ConsumerFactory;
 13	import org.springframework.kafka.core.DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory;
 14	
 15	import java.util.HashMap;
 16	import java.util.Map;
 17	
 18	@EnableKafka
 19	@Configuration
 20	public class KafkaConfig {
 21	
 22	 @Value("${spring.kafka.consumer.bootstrap-servers}")
 23	 private String bootstrapServers;
 24	
 25	 @Bean
 26	 public ConsumerFactory<String, UserEvent> consumerFactory() {
 27	 Map<String, Object> props = new HashMap<>();
 28	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);
 29	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, "auth-service-group");
 30	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);
 31	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, JsonDeserializer.class);
 32	 props.put(JsonDeserializer.TRUSTED_PACKAGES, "*");
 33	 return new DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory<>(props);
 34	 }
 35	
 36	 @Bean
 37	 public ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<String, UserEvent> kafkaListenerContainerFactory() {
 38	 ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<String, UserEvent> factory = new ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<>();
 39	 factory.setConsumerFactory(consumerFactory());
 40	 return factory;
 41	 }
 42	}

 20	
 21	 try {
 22	 switch (event.getEventType()) {
 23	 case "CREATE":

 28	 break;
 29	 case "DELETE":

 31	 break;
 32	 default:

 34	 }
 35	 } catch (Exception e) {
 36	 log.error("Error processing user event: {}", event, e);
 37	 // Implement error handling strategy (retry, DLQ, etc.)
 38	 }
 39	 }

You've identified the core issue perfectly. The problem is in line 26 of your auth-service.yml:

````yaml path=services/config-server/src/main/resources/configurations/auth-service.yml mode=EXCERPT
spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
````

This configuration is telling the deserializer: "When you see a message with type `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent`, map it to `org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent`". But the auth-service has **no knowledge** of `org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent` class!

## **The Correct Solutions:**

### **Solution 1: Remove Type Mapping Completely (Simplest)**

Since both your `UserEvent` classes have identical structure, just remove the type mapping and use the default type:

**Auth Service Configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 # Remove this line completely:
 # spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

**User Service Configuration:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

### **Solution 2: Use Custom Type Mapping with Alias (More Advanced)**

**User Service - Send with custom type alias:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: true
 spring.json.type.mapping: "org.endipi.user.producer.data.UserEvent:userEvent"
```

**Auth Service - Map alias to local class:**
```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 properties:
 spring.json.use.type.headers: true
 spring.json.type.mapping: "userEvent:org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent"
```

### **Solution 3: Shared Event Library (Best Practice)**

Create a separate `events-common` module that both services depend on:

```
events-common/
 └── src/main/java/org/endipi/events/UserEvent.java
```

Both services would then use the same `UserEvent` class.

## **Recommended Fix (Solution 1):**

Update your `auth-service.yml`:

```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 consumer:
 bootstrap-servers: localhost:9092
 group-id: auth-service-group
 key-deserializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.ErrorHandlingDeserializer
 value-deserializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.ErrorHandlingDeserializer
 properties:
 spring.json.trusted.packages: "*"
 spring.json.use.type.headers: false
 spring.json.value.default.type: org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent
 spring.deserializer.key.delegate.class: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
 spring.deserializer.value.delegate.class: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonDeserializer
```

And update your `user-service.yml`:

```yaml
spring:
 kafka:
 producer:
 bootstrap-servers: localhost:9092
 key-serializer: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
 value-serializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonSerializer
 properties:
 spring.json.add.type.headers: false
```

This way, the producer sends pure JSON without type information, and the consumer always deserializes it to its local `UserEvent` class.

Would you like to try Solution 1 first since it's the simplest?

This log shows that your auth-service is now starting up successfully and connecting to Kafka properly. Let me analyze what's happening:

## **Positive Signs from the Log:**

1. **Kafka Connection Successful**: 
 ```
 Cluster ID: J1sY9Sd4QfGuDkunXAtAwA
 ```

2. **Consumer Group Registration Working**:
 ```
 Discovered group coordinator localhost:9092
 (Re-)joining group
 ```

3. **Topic Subscription Successful**:
 ```
 Subscribed to topic(s): user-events
 ```

4. **No More Serialization Errors**: The previous `SerializationException` and `IllegalStateException` are gone!

## **What This Means:**

The auth-service consumer is now properly configured and waiting for messages. The "(Re-)joining group" messages are **normal Kafka behavior** - this happens when:

1. A new consumer joins a consumer group
2. Kafka is rebalancing partitions among consumers
3. The consumer is establishing its position in the group

## **Next Steps to Test:**

## **What This Means:**

The auth-service consumer is now properly configured and waiting for messages. The "(Re-)joining group" messages are **normal Kafka behavior** - this happens when:

2. Kafka is rebalancing partitions among consumers
3. The consumer is establishing its position in the group

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"1ba49335-e4e8-41bb-b3f5-c51444ee8227","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"**Found the problem!** 

You have **conflicting configurations**:

1. **YAML configuration** (auth-service.yml) - Uses `ErrorHandlingDeserializer` with delegate deserializers
2. **Java configuration** (KafkaConfig.java) - Uses direct `JsonDeserializer`

The Java `@Configuration` is **overriding** your YAML settings, and the Java config doesn't have the proper settings for handling the type mapping issue.

## **The Fix:**

You need to update your Java `KafkaConfig.java` to match your YAML approach. Here's what you should change:

**Option 1: Update Java Config (Recommended)**

```java
@Bean
public ConsumerFactory<String, UserEvent> consumerFactory() {
 Map<String, Object> props = new HashMap<>();
 props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);
 props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, "auth-service-group");
 
 // Use ErrorHandlingDeserializer like in YAML
 props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, ErrorHandlingDeserializer.class);
 props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, ErrorHandlingDeserializer.class);
 
 // Delegate to actual deserializers
 props.put(ErrorHandlingDeserializer.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS, StringDeserializer.class);
 props.put(ErrorHandlingDeserializer.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS, JsonDeserializer.class);
 
 // JSON deserializer settings
 props.put(JsonDeserializer.TRUSTED_PACKAGES, "*");
 props.put(JsonDeserializer.USE_TYPE_HEADERS, false);
 props.put(JsonDeserializer.VALUE_DEFAULT_TYPE, UserEvent.class);
 
 return new DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory<>(props);
}
```

**Option 2: Remove Java Config (Simpler)**

Just delete the entire `KafkaConfig.java` file and let Spring Boot use your YAML configuration automatically.

**Option 3: Fix YAML typo and remove Java config**

1. Fix the typo: `flase` → `false` in auth-service.yml line 25
2. Delete `KafkaConfig.java`

I recommend **Option 3** because it's the simplest and your YAML config is almost correct.

Which approach would you like to try?

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;

 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 6	import org.springframework.http.HttpMethod;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 8	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity;
 9	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.configuration.EnableWebSecurity;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.configurers.AbstractHttpConfigurer;
 11	import org.springframework.security.config.http.SessionCreationPolicy;
 12	import org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain;
 13	
 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 59	switch -wildcard -casesensitive ( $($distributionUrl -replace '^.*/','') ) {
 60	 "maven-mvnd-*" {
 61	 $USE_MVND = $true
 62	 $distributionUrl = $distributionUrl -replace '-bin\.[^.]*$',"-windows-amd64.zip"
 63	 $MVN_CMD = "mvnd.cmd"
 64	 break
 65	 }
 66	 default {
 67	 $USE_MVND = $false
 68	 $MVN_CMD = $script -replace '^mvnw','mvn'
 69	 break
 70	 }
 71	}
 72	
 73	# apply MVNW_REPOURL and calculate MAVEN_HOME
 74	# maven home pattern: ~/.m2/wrapper/dists/{apache-maven-<version>,maven-mvnd-<version>-<platform>}/<hash>
 75	if ($env:MVNW_REPOURL) {
 76	 $MVNW_REPO_PATTERN = if ($USE_MVND) { "/org/apache/maven/" } else { "/maven/mvnd/" }
 77	 $distributionUrl = "$env:MVNW_REPOURL$MVNW_REPO_PATTERN$($distributionUrl -replace '^.*'+$MVNW_REPO_PATTERN,'')"
 78	}
 79	$distributionUrlName = $distributionUrl -replace '^.*/',''
 80	$distributionUrlNameMain = $distributionUrlName -replace '\.[^.]*$','' -replace '-bin$',''
 81	$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$HOME/.m2/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 82	if ($env:MAVEN_USER_HOME) {
 83	 $MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$env:MAVEN_USER_HOME/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 84	}
 85	$MAVEN_HOME_NAME = ([System.Security.Cryptography.MD5]::Create().ComputeHash([byte[]][char[]]$distributionUrl) | ForEach-Object {$_.ToString("x2")}) -join ''
 86	$MAVEN_HOME = "$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT/$MAVEN_HOME_NAME"
 87	
 88	if (Test-Path -Path "$MAVEN_HOME" -PathType Container) {
 89	 Write-Verbose "found existing MAVEN_HOME at $MAVEN_HOME"
 90	 Write-Output "MVN_CMD=$MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD"
 91	 exit $?
 92	}

 28	# MVNW_USERNAME/MVNW_PASSWORD - user and password for downloading maven
 29	# MVNW_VERBOSE - true: enable verbose log; debug: trace the mvnw script; others: silence the output
 30	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

 107	
 108	# parse distributionUrl and optional distributionSha256Sum, requires .mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties
 109	while IFS="=" read -r key value; do
 110	 case "${key-}" in
 111	 distributionUrl) distributionUrl=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 112	 distributionSha256Sum) distributionSha256Sum=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 113	 esac
 114	done <"${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"
 115	[ -n "${distributionUrl-}" ] || die "cannot read distributionUrl property in ${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"
 116	
 117	case "${distributionUrl##*/}" in
 118	maven-mvnd-*bin.*)
 119	 MVN_CMD=mvnd.sh _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/maven/mvnd/
 120	 case "${PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE-}${PROCESSOR_ARCHITEW6432-}:$(uname -a)" in
 121	 *AMD64:CYGWIN* | *AMD64:MINGW*) distributionPlatform=windows-amd64 ;;
 122	 :Darwin*x86_64) distributionPlatform=darwin-amd64 ;;
 123	 :Darwin*arm64) distributionPlatform=darwin-aarch64 ;;
 124	 :Linux*x86_64*) distributionPlatform=linux-amd64 ;;
 125	 *)
 126	 echo "Cannot detect native platform for mvnd on $(uname)-$(uname -m), use pure java version" >&2
 127	 distributionPlatform=linux-amd64
 128	 ;;
 129	 esac
 130	 distributionUrl="${distributionUrl%-bin.*}-$distributionPlatform.zip"
 131	 ;;
 132	maven-mvnd-*) MVN_CMD=mvnd.sh _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/maven/mvnd/ ;;
 133	*) MVN_CMD="mvn${0##*/mvnw}" _MVNW_REPO_PATTERN=/org/apache/maven/ ;;
 134	esac
 135	
 136	# apply MVNW_REPOURL and calculate MAVEN_HOME
 137	# maven home pattern: ~/.m2/wrapper/dists/{apache-maven-<version>,maven-mvnd-<version>-<platform>}/<hash>
 138	[ -z "${MVNW_REPOURL-}" ] || distributionUrl="$MVNW_REPOURL$_MVNW_REPO_PATTERN${distributionUrl#*"$_MVNW_REPO_PATTERN"}"
 139	distributionUrlName="${distributionUrl##*/}"
 140	distributionUrlNameMain="${distributionUrlName%.*}"
 141	distributionUrlNameMain="${distributionUrlNameMain%-bin}"
 142	MAVEN_USER_HOME="${MAVEN_USER_HOME:-${HOME}/.m2}"
 143	MAVEN_HOME="${MAVEN_USER_HOME}/wrapper/dists/${distributionUrlNameMain-}/$(hash_string "$distributionUrl")"
 144	
 145	exec_maven() {
 146	 unset MVNW_VERBOSE MVNW_USERNAME MVNW_PASSWORD MVNW_REPOURL || :
 147	 exec "$MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD" "$@" || die "cannot exec $MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD"
 148	}

 1	package org.endipi.auth.resource;
 2	
 3	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;
 4	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 5	import org.endipi.auth.dto.request.AuthRequest;
 6	import org.endipi.auth.dto.request.ChangePasswordRequest;
 7	import org.endipi.auth.dto.response.ApiResponse;
 8	import org.endipi.auth.dto.response.AuthResponse;
 9	import org.endipi.auth.service.AuthService;
 10	import org.endipi.auth.util.JwtUtil;
 11	import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
 12	import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
 13	import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
 14	
 15	import java.util.Map;
 16	
 17	@RestController
 18	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 19	@RequestMapping("/api/v1/auth")
 20	public class AuthResource {
 21	 private final AuthService authenticationService;
 22	 private final JwtUtil jwtService;
 23	
 24	 @PostMapping("/login")
 25	 public AuthResponse login(@RequestBody(required = false) AuthRequest authRequest, HttpServletResponse response) {
 26	 return authenticationService.login(authRequest, response);
 27	 }
 28	
 29	 @PostMapping("/logout")
 30	 public ResponseEntity<?> logout(
 31	 @RequestHeader(name = "Authorization", required = false) String authorizationHeader,
 32	 @CookieValue(name = "refreshToken", required = false) String refreshToken,
 33	 HttpServletResponse response) {
 34	
 35	 String accessToken = null;
 36	 if (authorizationHeader != null && authorizationHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
 37	 accessToken = authorizationHeader.substring(7);
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 authenticationService.logout(accessToken, refreshToken, response);
 41	 return ResponseEntity.ok().body(Map.of("message", "Logged out successfully"));
 42	 }
 43	
 44	 @PostMapping("/introspect")
 45	 public ResponseEntity<?> introspect(@RequestHeader(value = "Authorization", required = false) String authorizationHeader) {
 46	 String accessToken = null;
 47	 if (authorizationHeader != null && authorizationHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
 48	 accessToken = authorizationHeader.substring(7);
 49	 }
 50	
 51	 boolean isValid = jwtService.isTokenValid(accessToken);
 52	 return ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of("valid", isValid));
 53	 }
 54	
 55	 @PostMapping("/refresh")
 56	 public ResponseEntity<?> refresh(@CookieValue(name = "refreshToken", required = false) String refreshToken,
 57	 HttpServletResponse response) {
 58	 if (refreshToken == null) {
 59	 return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED).body(Map.of("message", "Refresh token missing"));
 60	 }
 61	
 62	 return ResponseEntity.ok(authenticationService.refresh(refreshToken, response));
 63	 }
 64	
 65	 @PutMapping("/change-password")
 66	 public ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<String, String>> changePassword(
 67	 @RequestBody ChangePasswordRequest request,
 68	 @RequestHeader("Authorization") String token
 69	 ) {
 70	 token = token.substring(7);
 71	 String email = jwtService.extractUsername(token);
 72	
 73	 authenticationService.changePassword(email, request);
 74	 ApiResponse<String, String> response = ApiResponse.<String, String>builder()
 75	 .timestamp(System.currentTimeMillis())
 76	 .statusCode(HttpStatus.OK.value())
 77	 .message("Đổi mật khẩu thành công!")
 78	 .build();
 79	 return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.OK).body(response);
 80	 }

 82	}...

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.endipi.auth.repository.UserRepository;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 6	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
 8	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 9	import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
 11	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
 12	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException;
 13	import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
 14	import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
 15	
 16	@Configuration
 17	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 18	public class AppConfig {
 19	 private final UserRepository userRepository;
 20	
 21	 @Bean
 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {
 23	 return username -> userRepository.findByEmail(username)
 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}

 13	
 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 37	 @Override
 38	 public AuthResponse login(AuthRequest request, HttpServletResponse response) {
 39	 if (request == null || request.getEmail() == null || request.getPassword() == null
 40	 || request.getEmail().isEmpty() || request.getPassword().isEmpty()) {
 41	 throw new ApplicationException(ErrorCode.INVALID_LOGIN_REQUEST);
 42	 }
 43	
 44	 authenticationManager.authenticate(
 45	 new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(
 46	 request.getEmail(),
 47	 request.getPassword()
 48	 )
 49	 );
 50	
 51	 String email = request.getEmail();
 52	 User user = userRepository.findByEmail(email)
 53	 .orElseThrow(() -> new ApplicationException(ErrorCode.USER_NOT_FOUND));
...
 126	
 127	 Map<String, Object> extraClaims = new HashMap<>();
 128	 extraClaims.put("role", user.getRole().getRoleTitle());
 129	
 130	 String newAccessToken = jwtUtil.generateToken(extraClaims, user, 900000L);
 131	 String newRefreshToken = jwtUtil.generateToken(user, 604800000L);
 132	
 133	 // Blacklist old refresh token
 134	 blacklistedTokenRepository.save(BlacklistedToken.builder()
 135	 .token(refreshToken)
 136	 .tokenType(TokenType.REFRESH)
 137	 .expiryDate(Instant.now().plusSeconds(604800))
 138	 .build());
 139	
 140	 // Set new refresh token in cookie
 141	 setRefreshTokenCookie(response, newRefreshToken);
 142	
 143	 return AuthResponse.builder()
 144	 .accessToken(newAccessToken)
 145	 .build();
 146	 }

Path: services/auth/src/main/java/org/endipi/auth/config/AppConfig.java
 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.endipi.auth.repository.UserRepository;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 6	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
 8	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 9	import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
 11	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
 12	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException;
 13	import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
 14	import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
 15	
 16	@Configuration
 17	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 18	public class AppConfig {
 19	 private final UserRepository userRepository;
 20	
 21	 @Bean
 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {
 23	 return username -> userRepository.findByEmail(username)
 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}
...

 14	@Configuration
 15	@EnableWebSecurity
 16	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 17	public class SecurityConfig {
 18	 private final AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider;
 19	
 20	 @Bean
 21	 public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }
 34	}

 79	$distributionUrlName = $distributionUrl -replace '^.*/',''
 80	$distributionUrlNameMain = $distributionUrlName -replace '\.[^.]*$','' -replace '-bin$',''
 81	$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$HOME/.m2/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 82	if ($env:MAVEN_USER_HOME) {
 83	 $MAVEN_HOME_PARENT = "$env:MAVEN_USER_HOME/wrapper/dists/$distributionUrlNameMain"
 84	}
 85	$MAVEN_HOME_NAME = ([System.Security.Cryptography.MD5]::Create().ComputeHash([byte[]][char[]]$distributionUrl) | ForEach-Object {$_.ToString("x2")}) -join ''
 86	$MAVEN_HOME = "$MAVEN_HOME_PARENT/$MAVEN_HOME_NAME"
 87	
 88	if (Test-Path -Path "$MAVEN_HOME" -PathType Container) {
 89	 Write-Verbose "found existing MAVEN_HOME at $MAVEN_HOME"
 90	 Write-Output "MVN_CMD=$MAVEN_HOME/bin/$MVN_CMD"
 91	 exit $?
 92	}

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
 4	import org.endipi.auth.repository.AuthUserRepository;
 5	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 6	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 7	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
 8	import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider;
 9	import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
 10	import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
 11	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
 12	import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UsernameNotFoundException;
 13	import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
 14	import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
 15	
 16	@Configuration
 17	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 18	public class AppConfig {
 19	 private final AuthUserRepository authUserRepository;
 20	
 21	 @Bean
 22	 public UserDetailsService userDetailsService() {
 23	 return username -> authUserRepository.findByEmail(username)
 24	 .orElseThrow(() -> new UsernameNotFoundException("User not found"));
 25	 }
 26	
 27	 @Bean
 28	 public AuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
 29	 DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
 30	 authenticationProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService());
 31	 authenticationProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
 32	 return authenticationProvider;
 33	 }
 34	
 35	 @Bean
 36	 public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
 37	 return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
 38	 }
 39	
 40	 @Bean
 41	 public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
 42	 return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
 43	 }
 44	}
...

 20	 @Bean

 22	 http
 23	 .csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
 24	 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
 25	 .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.OPTIONS, "/**").permitAll()
 26	 .anyRequest().permitAll()
 27	 )
 28	 .sessionManagement(session -> session
 29	 .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)
 30	 )
 31	 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider);
 32	 return http.build();
 33	 }

 9	
 10	@Component
 11	@RequiredArgsConstructor
 12	public class UserEventProducer {
 13	 private final KafkaTemplate<String, Object> kafkaTemplate;
 14	
 15	 @Value("${kafka.topic.user-events}")
 16	 private String userEventsTopic;
 17	
 18	 public void publishUserCreated(User user) {
 19	 UserEvent event = UserEvent.builder()
 20	 .eventType("CREATE")
 21	 .userId(user.getId())
 22	 .email(user.getEmail())
 23	 .password(user.getPassword())
 24	 .role(user.getRole().getRoleTitle().name())
 25	 .timestamp(System.currentTimeMillis())
 26	 .build();
 27	
 28	 kafkaTemplate.send(userEventsTopic, user.getId().toString(), event);
 29	 }

 107	
 108	# parse distributionUrl and optional distributionSha256Sum, requires .mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties
 109	while IFS="=" read -r key value; do
 110	 case "${key-}" in
 111	 distributionUrl) distributionUrl=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 112	 distributionSha256Sum) distributionSha256Sum=$(trim "${value-}") ;;
 113	 esac
 114	done <"${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"
 115	[ -n "${distributionUrl-}" ] || die "cannot read distributionUrl property in ${0%/*}/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties"

 182	__MVNW_QUIET_WGET=--quiet __MVNW_QUIET_CURL=--silent __MVNW_QUIET_UNZIP=-q __MVNW_QUIET_TAR=''
 183	[ "${MVNW_VERBOSE-}" != true ] || __MVNW_QUIET_WGET='' __MVNW_QUIET_CURL='' __MVNW_QUIET_UNZIP='' __MVNW_QUIET_TAR=v
 184	
 185	# normalize http auth
 186	case "${MVNW_PASSWORD:+has-password}" in
 187	'') MVNW_USERNAME='' MVNW_PASSWORD='' ;;
 188	has-password) [ -n "${MVNW_USERNAME-}" ] || MVNW_USERNAME='' MVNW_PASSWORD='' ;;
 189	esac

 1	package org.endipi.user.config;
 2	
 3	import org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonSerializer;
 4	import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer;
 5	import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.ProducerConfig;
 6	import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;
 7	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 8	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 9	import org.springframework.kafka.annotation.EnableKafka;
 10	import org.springframework.kafka.core.DefaultKafkaProducerFactory;
 11	import org.springframework.kafka.core.KafkaTemplate;
 12	import org.springframework.kafka.core.ProducerFactory;
 13	
 14	import java.util.HashMap;
 15	import java.util.Map;
 16	
 17	@EnableKafka
 18	@Configuration
 19	public class KafkaConfig {
 20	 @Value("${spring.kafka.producer.bootstrap-servers}")
 21	 private String bootstrapServers;
 22	
 23	 @Bean
 24	 public ProducerFactory<String, Object> producerFactory() {
 25	 Map<String, Object> props = new HashMap<>();
 26	 props.put(ProducerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);
 27	 props.put(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringSerializer.class);
 28	 props.put(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, JsonSerializer.class);
 29	 return new DefaultKafkaProducerFactory<>(props);
 30	 }
 31	
 32	 @Bean
 33	 public KafkaTemplate<String, Object> kafkaTemplate() {
 34	 return new KafkaTemplate<>(producerFactory());
 35	 }
 36	}

 1	package org.endipi.auth.config;
 2	
 3	import org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonDeserializer;
 4	import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer;
 5	import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerConfig;
 6	import org.endipi.auth.consumer.data.UserEvent;
 7	import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;
 8	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
 9	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
 10	import org.springframework.kafka.annotation.EnableKafka;
 11	import org.springframework.kafka.config.ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory;
 12	import org.springframework.kafka.core.ConsumerFactory;
 13	import org.springframework.kafka.core.DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory;
 14	
 15	import java.util.HashMap;
 16	import java.util.Map;
 17	
 18	@EnableKafka
 19	@Configuration
 20	public class KafkaConfig {
 21	
 22	 @Value("${spring.kafka.consumer.bootstrap-servers}")
 23	 private String bootstrapServers;
 24	
 25	 @Bean
 26	 public ConsumerFactory<String, UserEvent> consumerFactory() {
 27	 Map<String, Object> props = new HashMap<>();
 28	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);
 29	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, "auth-service-group");
 30	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);
 31	 props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, JsonDeserializer.class);
 32	 props.put(JsonDeserializer.TRUSTED_PACKAGES, "*");
 33	 return new DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory<>(props);
 34	 }
 35	
 36	 @Bean
 37	 public ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<String, UserEvent> kafkaListenerContainerFactory() {
 38	 ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<String, UserEvent> factory = new ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory<>();
 39	 factory.setConsumerFactory(consumerFactory());
 40	 return factory;
 41	 }
 42	}
